content_en:
Tuhfat al-Muhtaj covers the complete range of Shafi'i fiqh with exceptional thoroughness. In the chapters on worship, Ibn Hajar is particularly careful to address the conditions for validity versus the elements whose omission merely makes the act deficient. His treatment of the prayer — its pillars, its obligatory elements, and the actions whose performance within the prayer is prohibited — is among the clearest systematic presentations in the Shafi'i literature. He addresses the prayer of the person in doubt about their purity state, the prayer behind an imam whose legal status is unknown, and the prayer of the traveler with careful attention to the textual sources.

In the law of transactions, the Tuhfa reflects the commercial environment of the Hijaz in the Ottoman period, where trade from across the Muslim world converged. The chapters on sale address complex scenarios involving deferred payment, goods in transit, defects discovered after purchase, and disputes between buyer and seller. The chapter on sarf (currency exchange) is detailed and practically oriented, addressing the conditions for valid exchange of different types of currency. The treatment of financial instruments used by merchants — bills of exchange, agency agreements, letters of credit — reflects the commercial sophistication of Meccan trade.

The family law chapters contain some of the Tuhfa's most careful analysis. Ibn Hajar's treatment of the mahr (dower) is detailed and addresses the many situations in which the amount, form, or payment of the mahr becomes disputed. His discussion of polygyny addresses the conditions under which a husband may take additional wives and the rights of the wives in terms of equitable treatment and residence. The chapters on divorce work through the various forms — talaq, khul', ila', zihar — with attention to the procedural requirements and the legal consequences of each.

The Tuhfa's treatment of the law of non-Muslims (ahl al-dhimma) under Islamic rule reflects the Hijazi context, where the Muslim community governed a territory that attracted traders and pilgrims of many backgrounds. Ibn Hajar's careful analysis of the rights and obligations of non-Muslim subjects provides important evidence for the history of Islamic legal pluralism.
content_id:
Tuhfat al-Muhtaj mencakup seluruh rentang fikih Syafi'i dengan ketuntasan yang luar biasa. Dalam bab-bab ibadah, Ibn Hajar sangat berhati-hati dalam membahas syarat-syarat keabsahan versus unsur-unsur yang penghilangnya hanya membuat ibadah menjadi tidak sempurna. Perlakuannya terhadap shalat — rukun-rukunnya, unsur-unsur wajibnya, dan tindakan-tindakan yang pelaksanaannya dalam shalat dilarang — termasuk yang paling jelas dalam presentasi sistematis dalam literatur Syafi'i. Ia membahas shalat orang yang ragu tentang keadaan kesuciannya, shalat di belakang imam yang status hukumnya tidak diketahui, dan shalat musafir dengan perhatian cermat terhadap sumber-sumber tekstual.

Dalam hukum transaksi, Tuhfah mencerminkan lingkungan perdagangan Hijaz di masa Utsmaniyah, di mana perdagangan dari seluruh dunia Muslim bertemu. Bab-bab tentang jual beli membahas skenario-skenario kompleks yang melibatkan pembayaran tertunda, barang dalam transit, cacat yang ditemukan setelah pembelian, dan perselisihan antara pembeli dan penjual. Bab tentang sharf (penukaran mata uang) terperinci dan berorientasi praktis, membahas syarat-syarat untuk pertukaran berbagai jenis mata uang yang sah. Perlakuan terhadap instrumen keuangan yang digunakan oleh pedagang — surat wesel, perjanjian keagenan, surat kredit — mencerminkan kecanggihan perdagangan Mekah.

Bab-bab hukum keluarga mengandung beberapa analisis Tuhfah yang paling cermat. Perlakuan Ibn Hajar terhadap mahar terperinci dan membahas banyak situasi di mana jumlah, bentuk, atau pembayaran mahar menjadi diperdebatkan. Diskusinya tentang poligini membahas kondisi-kondisi di mana suami dapat mengambil istri tambahan dan hak-hak para istri dalam hal perlakuan yang adil dan tempat tinggal. Bab-bab tentang talak membahas berbagai bentuknya — talak, khul', ila', zihar — dengan perhatian pada persyaratan prosedural dan konsekuensi hukum dari masing-masing.

Perlakuan Tuhfah terhadap hukum non-Muslim (ahl al-dzimmah) di bawah pemerintahan Islam mencerminkan konteks Hijaz, di mana komunitas Muslim memerintah wilayah yang menarik pedagang dan peziarah dari berbagai latar belakang. Analisis cermat Ibn Hajar tentang hak-hak dan kewajiban-kewajiban subjek non-Muslim memberikan bukti penting bagi sejarah pluralisme hukum Islam.
content_ar: Tuhfat al-Muhtaj covers the complete range of الشافعي الفقه with exceptional thoroughness. In the الأبواب on worship, Ibn Hajar is particularly careful to address the conditions for validity versus the elements whose omission merely makes the act deficient. His treatment of the الصلاة — its pillars, its obligatory elements, and the actions whose performance within the الصلاة is prohibited — is among the clearest systematic presentations in the الشافعي literature. He addresses the الصلاة of the person in doubt about their purity state, the الصلاة behind an الإمام whose legal status is unknown, and the الصلاة of the traveler with careful attention to the textual sources. In the law of transactions, the Tuhfa reflects the commercial environment of the Hijaz in the Ottoman period, where trade from across the مسلم الدنيا converged. The الأبواب on sale address complex scenarios involving deferred payment, goods in transit, defects discovered بعد purchase, and disputes between buyer and seller. The الباب on sarf (currency exchange) is detailed and practically oriented, addressing the conditions for valid exchange of different types of currency. The treatment of financial instruments used by merchants — bills of exchange, agency agreements, letters of credit — reflects the commercial sophistication of Meccan trade. The الأسرة law الأبواب contain some of the Tuhfa's most careful analysis. Ibn Hajar's treatment of the mahr (dower) is detailed and addresses the many situations in which the amount, form, or payment of the mahr becomes disputed. His discussion of polygyny addresses the conditions under which a الزوج may take additional wives and the الحقوق of the wives in terms of equitable treatment and residence. The الأبواب on divorce work through the various forms — talaq, khul', ila', zihar — with attention to the procedural requirements and the legal consequences of each. The Tuhfa's treatment of the law of non-المسلمون (ahl al-dhimma) under Islamic rule reflects the Hijazi context, where the مسلم الجماعة governed a territory that attracted traders and pilgrims of many backgrounds. Ibn Hajar's careful analysis of the الحقوق and obligations of non-مسلم subjects provides المهم evidence for the history of Islamic legal pluralism.
